en_def: a deep, wide crack or gap in the ground; a very great difference between people, groups, or things
zh_def: 裂縫；深壑；（比喻）巨大的鴻溝
en_example: Think of Chaos as a kind of grand cosmic yawn, as in a yawning chasm or a yawning void.
en_example_chunks: Think of Chaos / as a kind of grand cosmic yawn, / as in a yawning chasm / or a yawning void.
zh_example: 把混沌想像成一種宇宙級的呵欠，就像一道張得老大的深壑或虛空。

en_example: In the beginning there was nothing but a formless void, waiting to be filled.
en_example_chunks: In the beginning / there was nothing / but a formless void, / waiting to be filled.
zh_example: 在最初，除了一片渾沌虛空外一無所有，靜待著被填滿。

en_def: a gradual decline into disorder; a measure of the disorder in a system
en_example: It calls this inevitable fate entropy: part of the great cycle from Chaos to order and back again to Chaos.
en_example_chunks: It calls this inevitable fate entropy: / part of the great cycle / from Chaos to order / and back again to Chaos.
zh_example: 科學稱這個無可避免的命運為「熵」：從混沌到秩序，再度回歸混沌的巨大循環的一部分。

en_def: come together to form one mass or whole
en_example: Your trousers began as chaotic atoms that somehow coalesced into matter that ordered itself over aeons into a living substance.
en_example_chunks: Your trousers began as chaotic atoms / that somehow coalesced into matter / that ordered itself over aeons / into a living substance.
zh_example: 你的褲子最初不過是雜亂無序的原子，不知怎地凝聚成物質，經過漫長歲月自我組織成有生命的物質。

en_def: being a part of a whole; a component part
en_example: When the sun explodes, all the constituent atoms of this world will return to cold Chaos.
en_example_chunks: When the sun explodes, / all the constituent atoms of this world / will return to cold Chaos.
zh_example: 當太陽爆炸時，這個世界所有構成的原子都將回歸冰冷的混沌。

en_def: an indefinitely long period of time; an age
en_example: Matter ordered itself over aeons into the living substance that eventually became you.
en_example_chunks: Matter ordered itself / over aeons / into the living substance / that eventually became you.
zh_example: 物質經過漫長歲月自我整理，逐漸形成了最終成為你的那些生命物質。

en_def: to make something appear as if by magic; (conjure up) to produce an idea or image in the mind
zh_def: 施法變出；（conjure up）喚起、想像出
en_example: Whether Chaos brought life out of nothing or conjured it up in some other way, I don't know.
en_example_chunks: Whether Chaos brought life out of nothing / or conjured it up in some other way, / I don't know.
zh_example: 混沌究竟是無中生有帶來生命，還是用其他方式變出了生命，我並不知道。

en_def: to enclose or cover something, as if in a sheath
en_example: The cotton plant was woven into the handsome stuff that sheathes your lovely legs.
en_example_chunks: The cotton plant was woven / into the handsome stuff / that sheathes your lovely legs.
zh_example: 這株棉花被織成了包覆你美腿的那種考究布料。

en_def: certain to happen or to have a particular outcome; fated
en_example: Science today agrees that everything is destined to return to Chaos.
en_example_chunks: Science today agrees / that everything is destined / to return to Chaos.
zh_example: 當代科學也認同，萬物終將註定回歸混沌。

en_def: of, from, or like a god; relating to a deity
en_example: Was Chaos a god – a divine being – or simply a state of nothingness?
en_example_chunks: Was Chaos a god / – a divine being – / or simply a state of nothingness?
zh_example: 混沌究竟是一位神——一個神聖的存在——還是僅僅是一種虛無的狀態？
